business.headline: SpiceJet fined Rs 1 lakh for misleading consent tactics, loyalty programme opt-insA consumer watchdog finds SpiceJet's booking interface nudged users into loyalty enrolment and promotional consent, raising fresh questions over how digital platforms secure customer approval.
Reliance Q1 profit falls 22% despite 25% jump in revenue. Here's whyReliance Industries posted a 22% drop in first-quarter net profit even as revenue rose sharply.

Founder's life is hard: $1.2 billion fintech CEO says bank rejected home loanDespite a long relationship with the lender, a strong credit score and significant assets, a fintech founder said a leading private sector bank rejected his home loan application.

Apple beats Nvidia to become world's most valuable companyApple's market capitalisation stood at around $4.88 trillion on Friday as its shares remained largely steady. Nvidia, meanwhile, slipped to around $4.86 trillion after its shares fell 3.5%.

Awareness, better policies can unlock India's tourism potential, say expertsSpeaking at the India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, tourism industry representatives said India should focus on promoting year-round travel, creating greater awareness about unique destinations and ensuring stable policies to support the sector.

When no asset looks an easy bet, do multi-asset allocation funds make sense?Equity markets have faced a sharp correction amid geopolitical tensions, while gold and silver, traditionally considered safe havens during periods of uncertainty, have also fallen sharply from the all-time highs they touched in January.

Fake bosses, real losses: Sebi warns of scammers posing as CEOs in rising boss scamSebi has warned companies about a rising 'boss scam' in which fraudsters impersonate senior executives to trick employees into transferring funds, and urged firms to strengthen their security mechanisms and enhance vigilance.

F1 could return to India in 2028 as government races to fix old tax hurdlesThe government has begun talks with stakeholders to revive Formula One in India by 2028. The push centres on fixing old tax and regulatory hurdles that derailed the Indian Grand Prix.

VFS Global continues to invest in AI to streamline visa application processingSpeaking at India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, Yummi Talwar, COO, South Asia, VFS Global, said visa demand has largely returned to normal after the post-pandemic surge.

Sensex ends nearly 1,000 points up: 3 reasons why stock market jumped todaySensex and Nifty surged on Friday as IT stocks, Reliance Industries and private banks led buying. The S&P BSE Sensex was up 953.76 points, or 1.24%, at 78,140.63 around 3:07 pm, after touching an intraday high of 78,282.55.

8th Pay Commission: How much HRA could you get under different fitment factors?The HRA debate comes at an important stage in the 8th Pay Commission process. It has been more than seven months since the Commission was constituted. The Commission visited Kolkata, West Bengal, on July 9 and 10, 2026, as part of its ongoing consultation exercise.

Your ITR could be wrong even with Form 16: 7 smart checks to avoid tax noticesThink your Form 16 is all you need to file your ITR? Think again. A few missed details could delay your refund, or even land you a tax notice.

Maruti challenges landmark E20 ruling, says contaminated fuel was the real issueMaruti Suzuki will challenge the Raipur consumer court order on a Grand Vitara E20 dispute. The case sharpens scrutiny of whether alleged damage came from ethanol-blended petrol or contaminated fuel.

Why investors are betting against Elon Musk's SpaceXSome investors are concerned about the company's heavy investment in artificial intelligence, much of which is being financed through debt. These concerns have added pressure on the stock in recent weeks.

EPFO plans PF scheme for self-employed and unorganised workersIf implemented, the proposed scheme could significantly expand India's social security system by giving millions of self-employed individuals and workers in the unorganised sector an opportunity to save for retirement through a trusted and structured provident fund mechanism.

Trump posts, markets move. His company now wants to turn that into a businessTrump Media plans to launch Truth PSI, a paid service offering early access to Truth Social posts. The proposal has prompted criticism over possible market advantage and conflict-of-interest concerns.

Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech rally 2%; Why are IT stocks rising today?Tech Mahindra's stronger June-quarter earnings lifted IT shares, with Infosys, TCS and HCLTech joining the rally while Wipro fell. The divergent moves highlighted improving confidence in stronger performers as investors watched the sector's earnings season.

Tata, SP Group in fresh talks on Tata Sons stake swapTata Sons and the SP Group are exploring a share swap to monetise part of the latter's stake. The talks matter because they could reduce SP Group debt, though valuation and structure remain unresolved.

Markets open higher as IT stocks surge; Reliance rises before Q1 resultsThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 456.40 points, or 0.59%, to 77,643.27, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 120.90 points, or 0.50%, to 24,193.65 as of 9:30 am.

SBI Funds Management IPO allotment: GMP at Rs 97. Here's how to check your statusSBI Funds Management IPO subscribed 41.73 times. Based on the upper price band of Rs 574 per share, the estimated listing price works out to around Rs 671, implying a potential listing gain of 16.90% if the current GMP trend holds.

entertainment.headline: Jennifer Winget's viral bridal gown clip fuels wedding rumours. Watch Wedding rumours surrounding Jennifer Winget have intensified after a behind-the-scenes video of her bridal gown fittings went viral on social media. The actor has not responded to reports claiming she married Singapore-based businessman William Ishmael in a private UK ceremony.

Don't book The Odyssey tickets before reading this IMAX movie format guide Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey was filmed entirely with IMAX cameras, but audiences can choose from several theatrical formats, including IMAX 70mm, Dolby Cinema and PLF. The film's official website explains the differences between each format to help viewers pick their preferred big-screen experience.

Assam govt approaches Aditya Dhar to make biopic on army general Lachit Borphukan Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma has reached out to Dhurandhar director Aditya Dhar for a biopic on Lachit Borphukan. Discussions on the project are currently underway.

Sonam Wangchuk thanks Sonakshi Sinha, Omi Vaidya for support amid hunger strike A message from Sonam Wangchuk's office appeared on Sonakshi Sinha and Omi Vaidya's posts after they shared messages of solidarity amid his hunger strike.

Rasika Dugal hopes Mirzapur's loyal OTT fans turn up in theatres Actor Rasika Dugal said returning to Mirzapur for its film version felt exciting and calmer. She said the move from streaming to cinemas and revisiting Beena Tripathi brought both curiosity and nerves.

Actor Murali Sharma on why Telugu cinema is moving beyond masala entertainers Veteran actor Murali Sharma says Telugu cinema has evolved beyond masala entertainers, with even top stars embracing socially relevant stories. Citing Nandamuri Balakrishna's Bhagavanth Kesari, he says such films have the power to influence audiences on a massive scale.

Oscar-winning actor Brenda Fricker, known for Home Alone 2, dies at 81 Oscar-winning Irish actor Brenda Fricker, best known for My Left Foot, Home Alone 2 and Casualty, died at the age of 81. She made history in 1990 as the first Irish female actor to win an Academy Award.

A new low: Internet reacts as Aamir says Wangchuk didn't inspire 3 Idiots' Wangdu Actor Aamir Khan's denial that Phunsukh Wangdu in 3 Idiots was inspired by Sonam Wangchuk has sparked a heated debate online. Social media users pointed to Wangchuk's old interviews and questioned the actor's claim that the filmmakers did not know the activist while making the film.

He wanted Akshay out of film: OMG 2 makers shred Paresh Rawal over story-theft claim The controversy around the story credit for OMG 2 intensified as producer Ashwin Varde refuted Paresh Rawal's claims. Varde accused Rawal of attempting to create a version of OMG without Akshay Kumar.

CM Vijay's birthday cake becomes a hit in Chennai, 400 sold in 2 weeks: Baker A cake from Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay's 52nd birthday celebration has become a hit in Chennai. The baker says she has sold 300 to 400 mango vanilla cakes, now popularly known as the 'CM Cake', after a birthday photo shared by Trisha drew attention to it.

They should have brought Kiran Bedi: Farah Khan on being called 'soft jailer' on Lock Upp Farah Khan said she is bound to get emotionally involved as Lock Upp's jailer. Her remarks came as she addressed bias allegations, praised contestants' honesty and underlined the show's emotional pull.

Old video of Rajkumar Hirani saying FTII man inspired 3 Idiots role goes viral An old video of filmmaker Rajkumar Hirani has gone viral after Aamir Khan denied that 3 Idiots' Phunsukh Wangdu was based on Sonam Wangchuk. In the resurfaced clip, Hirani says the character was inspired by a man who secretly studied at FTII using another person's admission.

RRR echoes at New York City Hall as Zohran Mamdani presents Rental Rip-off report Zohran Mamdani referenced SS Rajamouli's RRR while unveiling New York's Rental Rip-off Report at City Hall. The remark drew laughs in the room and prompted a reaction from the film's official X handle.

Old Sonam Wangchuk video surfaces after Aamir Khan says he didn't know him in 2009 An old video of activist Sonam Wangchuk recalling his 2008 meeting with Aamir Khan has resurfaced. The clip showed up after the actor denied any connection between Wangchuk and his 3 Idiots character Phunsukh Wangdu.

Booked tickets in 2025, watched Nolan film in 2026: The Odyssey obsession is real The Odyssey was released today. Some fans had booked IMAX 70 mm first-day tickets a year ago. The frenzy, mirrored by themed costumes and steep advance sales in India, underlined filmmaker Christopher Nolan's draw.

They saw what happened to me: Swara Bhasker on Bollywood skipping CJP protest At Jantar Mantar, Swara Bhasker said many in Bollywood avoided Sonam Wangchuk's protest out of fear. Her remarks came as Wangchuk's hunger strike entered a critical phase and renewed questions over public support.

The Odyssey ticket prices soar in India, premium seats cost up to Rs 3,400 Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is racing through advance bookings in India ahead of its July 17 release. Premium pricing, rave reviews and trade forecasts have set up a likely opening above Oppenheimer.

Why India, why now? The real reason behind Nolan bringing The Odyssey to India Director Christopher Nolan brought actors Matt Damon and Tom Holland to Mumbai for The Odyssey's India premiere. The visit underscored India's rising importance in the film's global campaign and India as a potential market for Nolan's films.

BTS' NORMAL is a quiet reflection on fame, friendship and finding normalcy BTS has unveiled their much-anticipated NORMAL music video, wrapping up an innovative teaser campaign that captured global attention. The understated release reframes fame through friendship, silence, and the search for ordinary moments.

general news.headline: US launches fresh airstrikes on Iran as campaign enters seventh night Live UpdatesThe US military launched a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran, saying the operations aim to weaken Iranian military capabilities. It also said US forces redirected, disabled and boarded vessels while enforcing a renewed naval blockade against Iran.

Lord's ODI will not be Rohit Sharma's last: BCCI secretary Devajit SaikiaBCCI secretary Devajit Saikia has dismissed speculation surrounding Rohit Sharma's ODI future, confirming that the former India captain will not retire after the third ODI against England at Lord's. The clarification comes after reports claimed that the Indian team management was planning to move on from the veteran opener ahead of the 2027 ODI World Cup.

India to blame for Bullet Train delay? Govt junks Japan ex-minister's claimFormer Japanese minister Hideki Makihara accused Indian authorities over the alleged delay in the Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train project. The minister in-charge of the negotiations was especially awful, alleged Makihara. India's foreign ministry rejected the allegations, saying talks were progressing well.

US weighs wider offensive against Iran, Trump seeks more refuelling jets in IsraelAs tensions flare up in West Asia, Trump plans to deploy dozens more aerial refuelling aircraft to Israel, as US reviews options for expanding military operations against Iran.

What B-schools are missing? A Messi playbook for corporate leadersFor years, Lionel Messi was judged by the one trophy that eluded him. Today, with another World Cup final on the horizon, the conversation has shifted from what he failed to win to how he transformed a team, a nation and the very idea of leadership.

Closely following developments: Govt on US proposal for 100% tariff on oil buyersAs Washington weighs a bill targeting countries that buy Russian crude, India responds cautiously, reiterating its energy sourcing approach while the proposed legislation gathers support in the US Senate.

Sir Garfield Sobers, one of cricket's greatest all-rounders, dies at 89Sir Garfield Sobers, widely regarded as cricket's greatest all-rounder, has died at the age of 89. The West Indies legend leaves behind an unmatched legacy, having redefined excellence with bat, ball and in the field across a glittering international career.

Will return as a ghost: Sonam Wangchuk gives July 20th condition for ending fastActivist Sonam Wangchuk, whose indefinite hunger strike entered its 20th day, has announced when he would end his fast. Wangchuk said he would end the fast on July 20 after participating in the march to Parliament. He joked that he would 'come back as a ghost' if there wasn't much participation in the march.

The film's official website explains the differences between each format to help viewers pick their preferred big-screen experience.

Trump wants to replace immigrant truckers in US. 1.5 lakh Indians to be hitUS President Donald Trump has proposed replacing immigrant truckers with American military veterans, alleging that many foreign drivers are intoxicated and responsible for killing motorists. The proposal, if implemented, could affect many Indian truckers in the US, particularly Punjabis and Sikhs.

US to replace 'duration of status' with fixed stay, Indian students face new uncertaintyA DHS proposal would replace duration-of-status rules with a fixed stay period for international students in the US. The change could complicate planning for Indian students, especially those on longer academic tracks.

Insult Vande Mataram, risk jail: What is the new bill headed to ParliamentThe new bill, to be introduced in Parliament next week, gives Vande Mataram the same legal protection as the national anthem, the tricolour and the Constitution. It is likely to spur another round of intense BJP vs Congress face-off over the song's history.

The Turkish toll template that Iran is replicating in Strait of HormuzIran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, but its bigger ambition is to regulate it. By adapting Turkey's straits model, Tehran hopes to turn the shipping chokepoint into a source of influence -- and billions in revenue.

When a death after 58-day hunger strike shook Nehru, gave birth to AndhraFive years after Independence, Gandhian leader and activist Potti Sriramulu began a fast until death demanding a separate state for Telugu-speaking people. His death after 58 days of fast forced PM Jawaharlal Nehru to act and declare the formation of the Andhra State, the first on linguistic lines.

Only those who marry once can live in Madhya Pradesh: Mohan Yadav's big UCC pitchMadhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav strongly pitched for Uniform Civil Code (UCC) implementation, saying only those who marry once would have the legal right to live in the state. He recently signalled that a UCC Bill could be introduced during the Assembly's Monsoon Session from July 20.

Why are top scientists leaving Isro? Money is not the only issueMore than 100 scientists have resigned or taken voluntary retirement from Isro, prompting the Department of Space to tighten exit rules for staff on flagship missions.

Video: Shashi Tharoor trapped in hotel lift, rescued by fire teamShashi Tharoor and several others were rescued after getting trapped in a hotel lift in Thiruvananthapuram. The Congress MP thanked the fire personnel and later felicitated them for their prompt response.

Mahesh Tutorials shuts 33 coaching centres in Maharashtra; 2,500 students affectedMahesh Tutorials has abruptly shut 33 coaching centres across Maharashtra, affecting around 2,500 students after admissions and fee collection for the academic year. The closure comes amid the long-running insolvency proceedings of parent company MT Educare, leaving students, parents and staff facing uncertainty over classes, fees and salaries.

India gets first hydrogen train: If it needs no electricity, how does it run?India has launched its first indigenously developed hydrogen fuel cell train on the Jind-Sonipat section. The pilot builds railway infrastructure and operational experience for wider green mobility.

Live: Iran says 3 killed in Hormozgan as tankers burn in Strait of HormuzUS-Iran War Live Updates: US-Iran tensions escalated further on Friday as American forces launched another round of airstrikes on military targets inside Iran, saying the strikes were aimed at eliminating "emerging threats" and degrading capabilities used to attack commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The fresh operation came ahead of a renewed US naval blockade on Iranian ports and coastal areas. Tehran responded by claiming it had struck military targets in Bahrain and Kuwait, including weapons depots and a drone launch ramp at Kuwait's Ali Al Salem Air Base, calling the attacks retaliation for recent US strikes. Kuwait later said it intercepted one ballistic missile, five cruise missiles and 33 drones, accusing Iran of targeting vital civilian and military facilities. One Kuwaiti Navy vessel was hit and four service members were injured. Iran also hardened its stance on the Strait of Hormuz, with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warning that "not a drop of oil and gas will be exported" from the region as long as US forces remain there, while a deputy foreign minister declared, "We will exercise our sovereignty on the Strait of Hormuz whatever that costs." Amid the military escalation, US President Donald Trump reversed his proposal to charge a 20 per cent fee on cargo transiting the strategic waterway just a day after announcing it. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates.

The Odyssey review: A timeless epic reborn through Christopher Nolan's geniusRelease Date:Fri Jul 17, 2026The Odyssey movie review: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey blends myth, emotion and breathtaking spectacle into one unforgettable journey. Anchored by Matt Damon's brilliant performance, it's a film that demands to be seen on the biggest screen.
Man strangles wife with dog leash, dies by suicide using same strap in BengaluruFollowing an argument, Manoj allegedly strangled his wife, Swapna, before dying by suicide inside their Bengaluru home.

When I see women like you...: Auto driver who made lewd remarks to woman gets bailA Mumbai court granted bail to an autorickshaw driver accused of making sexually explicit remarks to a woman passenger during a ride in Andheri. The court said there was no allegation of physical contact and imposed conditions, including stalking of the woman and tampering with evidence.

One's dream came from loss, another's from love for biology: Journey of NEET toppersNEET UG 2026 toppers Aryan Gupta and Panshul Bansal prove there is no single formula for success. One was inspired by personal loss, the other by a love for biology, but both reached the top through discipline, consistency and unwavering focus.

sports.headline: The greatest chapter of my career: Deschamps reflects before France farewell Didier Deschamps called the French national team the "greatest thing" of his professional career as he reflected on his 25-year journey with Les Bleus ahead of his final match in charge against England.

The other Lionel: How Scaloni made Argentina great again Lionel Messi's Argentina are in the FIFA World Cup final once again. But behind this golden era stands the other Lionel - Lionel Scaloni, the accidental manager who rebuilt Argentina, brought Messi back from the brink, and returned the glory days to the Albiceleste.

15-year-old shooter selected for national trials goes missing in Howrah Fifteen-year-old national rifle shooter Damayanti Sen has gone missing from Howrah under mysterious circumstances. Police have launched an investigation after the teenager, who recently qualified for national team trials, failed to return home.

Argentina will struggle to make a messy game vs Spain in World Cup final India Today's Consulting Editor Rajdeep Sardesai joins football experts Karim Bencherifa, Ranjeet Bajaj, Siddhant Aney and host Nikhil Naz for a special FIFA World Cup final preview. Can Lionel Messi inspire Argentina to glory, or will Spain's young stars complete their fairytale run? The panel discusses it all in this roundtable session.

Lord's ODI will not be Rohit Sharma's last: BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia has dismissed speculation surrounding Rohit Sharma's ODI future, confirming that the former India captain will not retire after the third ODI against England at Lord's. The clarification comes after reports claimed that the Indian team management was planning to move on from the veteran opener ahead of the 2027 ODI World Cup.

Chennai Grand Masters: World champion Gukesh stunned by Firouzja in Round 2 Alireza Firouzja outlasted world champion D Gukesh in 69 moves at the Chennai Grand Masters. The win gave the French Grandmaster sole lead after two rounds and underlined the ruthlessness of this field.

When Garry Sobers waited in the stands to meet Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli Sir Garfield Sobers' greatness was defined not just by his unmatched records but also by his humility. During India's 2023 tour of the West Indies, the cricket legend quietly walked up to Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli and Shubman Gill after practice, offering a lasting reminder of the man behind the game's greatest all-round legacy.

Sir Garfield Sobers, one of cricket's greatest all-rounders, dies at 89 Sir Garfield Sobers, widely regarded as cricket's greatest all-rounder, has died at the age of 89. The West Indies legend leaves behind an unmatched legacy, having redefined excellence with bat, ball and in the field across a glittering international career.

Don't make Rohit feel unwanted: Ashwin calls for lessons from his retirement Ravichandran Ashwin is not happy with how things are being handled in the Indian team. The former India spinner has said that if India did not want Rohit in the 2027 World Cup, the selectors should have told him this right after the Champions Trophy 2025.

Emma Raducanu urged to learn from Roger Federer amid worrying career slide Former British No.1 Greg Rusedski has urged Emma Raducanu to follow Roger Federer's example by maintaining greater consistency in her training and support system as she looks to overcome her injury struggles.

Rohit Sharma's retirement may force Virat Kohli to exit: Ex-Pakistan cricketer Former Pakistan batter Basit Ali has warned that forcing Rohit Sharma into retirement could also influence Virat Kohli's future, claiming that pressure on one senior star could have repercussions for the other.

FIFA to present NBA-styled championship rings to winners of World CupFIFA will present NBA-style championship rings to the winners of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, marking the first time the global governing body has introduced the American sporting tradition. Defending champions Argentina take on Spain in Sunday's final, with history on the line.

Andy Flower rules himself out of England role, will continue at RCBRCB head coach Andy Flower has ruled himself out of the race to become England's next Test coach. Speaking to The Telegraph, Flower said that he was happy with his current role in franchise cricket.

Former India U-19 star Manjot Kalra arrested in Lanka Premier League bribery caseFormer India Under-19 World Cup winner Manjot Kalra was arrested in Sri Lanka over allegations of attempting to bribe players during the Lanka Premier League, where he is a co-owner of the Jaffna Kings franchise.

What B-schools are missing? A Messi playbook for corporate leadersFor years, Lionel Messi was judged by the one trophy that eluded him. Today, with another World Cup final on the horizon, the conversation has shifted from what he failed to win to how he transformed a team, a nation and the very idea of leadership.

2nd T20I: Live Score and Updates from BulawayoZIM vs BAN, 2nd T20I: Zimbabwe have won the toss and opted to bowl first in Bulawayo. The hosts are unchanged after winning the series opener while Bangladesh have brought in Rishad Hossain for Nasum Ahmed.

No surprises as India bank on proven names for Women's Hockey World Cup 2026India have retained the same 20-player squad for the Women's Hockey World Cup, with Salima Tete to captain the side. India have opted for a perfect blend for youth and experience as they head to the Netherlands and Belgium.

Meet Keyne Yamal: Lamine's younger brother stealing the World Cup spotlightWhile Lamine Yamal prepares to face Lionel Messi in the World Cup final, his younger brother Keyne has become Spain's breakout figure from the stands. The three-year-old's antics and obvious bond with Lamine have made him the team's unofficial mascot during the tournament.

Pakistan's Mohammad Nawaz penalised after ICC anti-doping code breachPakistan all-rounder Mohammad Nawaz has accepted a sanction after testing positive for Carboxy-THC during the 2026 T20 World Cup, with his three-month ban set to be reduced upon completion of a treatment programme.

Fashionably late, ruthlessly on time: Argentina make comebacks a habitFor the third straight knockout game, Argentina waited until the dying moments to land the decisive blow. Their dramatic comeback against England was far more than just Lionel Messi's brilliance as it was another reminder of a team that thrives when the pressure is at its highest.

Parthiv Patel backs Rohit Sharma to bow out in style amid retirement talkFormer India wicketkeeper Parthiv Patel backed Rohit Sharma to silence his critics with a century at Lord's amid growing speculation that the series decider against England could be his final ODI appearance.

Bellingham shares team bus driver's letter with England fans after WC exitJude Bellingham thanked England supporters after the World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina, sharing a handwritten note from the team bus driver. Bellingham promised the fans that the team would bounce back and win major tournaments in the future.

Rohit Sharma's retirement speculation explained: Indian cricket set for messy weekendRohit Sharma's dream of playing one last ODI World Cup appears to have been cut short. Reports have emerged out of England that the selectors have decided to move on from the former India captain. But with Shubman Gill publicly backing Rohit just days earlier, Indian cricket finds itself caught in another messy episode of mixed messaging.

Vincic, referee for World Cup final, has a shocking past with Messi, ArgentinaFIFA has appointed Slavko Vincic to referee the World Cup final between Argentina and Spain. But the announcement will have the Albiceleste fans sweating as Vincic was the official for one of Argentina's shocking losses in recent times in the tournament.

PV Sindhu ends India's 15-year wait, reaches maiden Japan Open semisPV Sindhu reached her maiden Japan Open semi-final after Nozomi Okuhara withdrew from their quarter-final clash, ending India's 15-year wait for a women's singles semi-final appearance at the Super 750 event.

Spain wanted Messi. Argentina needed himLong before Lionel Messi became the face of Argentine football, Spain believed it had a chance to make him its own. As Argentina and Spain prepare to meet in the World Cup final, one of football's great what-ifs returns to the spotlight: what if Messi had chosen Spain?

Carlos Alcaraz finally returns to action, named in start list for Cincinnati OpenCarlos Alcaraz is set to return to the ATP Tour after a four-month injury layoff, having been named in the Cincinnati Open start list ahead of the US Open.

Watch: Slovenian referee moved to tears after World Cup final appointmentSlovenia's Slavko Vincic was appointed referee for the World Cup final between Argentina and Spain in New York. His tearful reaction to the announcement underlined what the assignment means beyond the touchline.

Smriti Mandhana ready for India captaincy. But is she good enough?In an exclusive interview with India Today, former India fast bowler Devika Palshikar weighed in on whether Smriti Mandhana possesses the qualities and leadership skills needed to captain the national team at the highest level.

No pressure on Rohit: Team India make stance clear as retirement buzz growsSitanshu Kotak said India are not putting Rohit Sharma under pressure despite the noise around his ODI future. Reports on Thursday suggested that the selectors are ready to move on from Rohit and he wasn't in the plans for the ODI World Cup next year.

No rift between Kohli and Gambhir: India batting coach slams rumoursIndia batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Virat Kohli and Gautam Gambhir were in regular conversation during the second ODI in Cardiff and dismissed rumours suggesting that there was a rift in the working relationship between the two.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
